dashboard.subtitle: Select local utility widgets, reports, and AI Created Widgets for the tools you want close at hand.
dashboard.backgroundDefaultHint: Uses the active color scheme background.
dashboard.backgroundDynamicHint: WARNING: some backgrounds may impact performance
dashboard.backgroundImageHint: Choose an image to use as this view's background.
dashboard.backgroundMediaHint: Choose an image or video to use as this view's background.
dashboard.backgroundMediaSourcePrefix: You can find high quality royalty free wallpapers at

dashboard.invalidScriptWidgetBody: Script widget body is not valid JSON.
dashboard.widgetSettingsEmpty: No custom settings for this widget.

dashboard.reportStep3: Capture terminal evidence only when explicitly needed.
dashboard.assistantContextIntro: The user is viewing this Dashboard view. Use the active widgets and reports as page context.
dashboard.assistantSummary: Browse and manage Dashboard widgets through the AI Assistant.
dashboard.playgroundHint: Add widgets from the catalog, then remove or replace them as your workflow changes.
dashboard.deleteViewBody: Delete "{{name}}" and all widgets in this Dashboard view? This cannot be undone.
dashboard.deleteWidgetBody: Remove "{{name}}" from this Dashboard view? This cannot be undone.
dashboard.connectionPaneSummary: Pin saved Connections into a live, interactive Dashboard pane.
dashboard.connectionWidgetNoConnectionsHint: Create a Connection in the Workspace module, then add it here.
dashboard.connectionWidgetEmptyHint: Add a saved Connection to show its live pane directly inside this widget.
dashboard.aiCodingUsageSummary: Monitor Codex and Claude Code usage windows from the Dashboard.
dashboard.aiCodingUsageSubtitle: Codex and Claude Code quota windows
dashboard.aiCodingUsageProviderHint: Connect this provider to start monitoring usage.
dashboard.aiCodingUsageEmptyHint: Click Add to add Codex or Claude Code to this widget.
dashboard.notesSummary: A post-it style note for quick reminders, with adjustable paper color and font.
dashboard.urlViewerSummary: Open a website with the native URL viewer and optional auto-reload.
dashboard.urlWidgetEmptyHint: Enter a URL to open it inside the Dashboard.
dashboard.deleteCustomWidgetBody: This deletes the AI Created Widget "{{name}}" and all its instances from every Dashboard view. This cannot be undone.
dashboard.importWidgetPasteHint: Paste a .kkwidget JSON export or load one from disk.
dashboard.importWidgetPreviewEmpty: Load or paste widget JSON to preview it before importing.
dashboard.emptyHint: Choose a widget from the catalog to build a focused Dashboard workspace.

subnetError.invalidFormat: Enter IPv4 CIDR notation, for example 192.168.10.44/27.
subnetError.invalidAddress: Each IPv4 octet must be between 0 and 255.

dashboard.quickToolsSummary: Encode, decode, and convert small operational strings.

quickToolErrors.invalidInput: The selected transform could not parse that input.
quickToolErrors.invalidNumber: Enter a Unix timestamp as seconds or milliseconds.

dashboard.reportSummary: A lightweight checklist report for routine operator review.
dashboard.reportBody:
1. Confirm current backup age.
2. Review changed Connections before maintenance.
3. Capture terminal evidence only when explicitly needed.
4. Keep command execution approval-based.
dashboard.agentWidgetDialogHint: Paste a reviewed widget JSON block from the AI Assistant or a coding agent. KKTerm saves it locally and does not run code.
dashboard.agentWidgetExample:
{
  "id": "agent-weekly-checklist",
  "title": "Weekly Ops Checklist",
  "category": "report",
  "summary": "A locally saved report widget.",
  "body": "Review backup status before maintenance windows."
}
dashboard.agentWidgetBuiltInId: Use a different widget id; that id belongs to a built-in widget.

agentWidgetErrors.invalidTitle: Add a title up to 64 characters.
agentWidgetErrors.invalidCategory: Use one category: hash, network, quick, or report.
agentWidgetErrors.invalidSummary: Add a summary up to 180 characters.

dashboard.hashInvalidInput: That input could not be decoded.

dashboard.dnsError: The lookup failed. Check the name and your connection.
dashboard.dnsNoRecords: No records of that type were found.
dashboard.speedtestNote: Runs only when you start it. Uses real bandwidth.
dashboard.networkToolsSummary: Subnet math, DNS, speed tests, ping sweeps, and WHOIS in one tabbed widget.

dashboard.pingHint: Paste targets, set the duration and interval, then start the run.
dashboard.pingDesktopOnly: Ping runs in the desktop app.
dashboard.whoisHint: Enter a domain to query public WHOIS records.
dashboard.whoisDesktopOnly: Whois runs in the desktop app.

dashboard.convertersSummary: Unit, currency, and image conversion in one widget.

imageConverter.selectHint: Choose a JPG, PNG, or WebP image.

imageConverter.dimensionLimit: Width and height must not exceed 16,384 px.
imageConverter.pixelLimit: The image must not exceed 50 megapixels.

dashboard.currencyEstimate: Rates are estimates. Fetch latest before relying on them.

options.scopeSelfElevatingHint: This installer may still show UAC in user mode.

itops.title: IT Ops
itops.subtitle: Fleet operations · Host Groups, Batch Runs & Automations

hostGroups.heading: Host Groups
hostGroups.emptyBody: A Host Group is a named selection of existing Connections used as a fleet target for Batch Runs and Automations.
hostGroups.perHostTransportHint: How a Batch Run reaches each host. Auto derives it from the Connection.
hostGroups.dynamicFilterHint: Resolved at run time — newly added Connections are picked up automatically.
hostGroups.noConnections: No Connections in this Workspace yet.
hostGroups.noMembers: No Connections match this Host Group yet.
hostGroups.deleteBody: “{{name}}” will be removed. Its Connections are not affected.

batchRuns.emptyBody: Send a script or an interactive playbook to every host in a Host Group at once, with per-host live output and a saved report.
batchRuns.savedReport: Consolidated report saved to run history · source manual · {{ok}}/{{total}} succeeded
batchRuns.scriptPlaceholder: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y
batchRuns.noGroups: Create a Host Group first to run a Batch Run.
batchRuns.stepSendPlaceholder: command or input, e.g. sudo systemctl restart nginx
batchRuns.stepExpectHint: Optional. Advance once this text appears in the output.

automations.emptyBody: A durable rule — one trigger, an optional condition, and ordered actions — that re-arms every time KKTerm launches. The evolved Watchdog.
automations.deleteBody: “{{name}}” will be removed and its Watchdog disarmed.
automations.noActionsHint: No actions yet — the Automation just records the trigger.
automations.cronHint: minute hour day month weekday, local time (e.g. 0 3 * * * = daily at 03:00)

editor.title: Edit Automation
editor.conditionHint: Fires when the sampled value matches this comparison.
editor.selectHint: Select a node to edit it, or add an action.
editor.noteSchedule: Schedule triggers fire when the cron expression matches the current minute, so a test rarely fires on demand.
editor.noteNeedsSession: This trigger samples a live Session; start the watched Session for a meaningful test value.

settings.title: Settings
settings.dashboardDescription: Configure default layout, widget behavior, and view management for the Dashboard.
settings.credentialsHint: Manage saved passwords, website credentials, AI provider keys, and widget secrets. Secret values stay in the selected secret store; KKTerm only shows metadata here.
settings.credentialStorageHint: Choose where KKTerm reads and writes secret values on this device.
settings.credentialStorageOsComparison: OS keystore uses the operating system's credential protection and unlocks with your signed-in account. Use it when you want native prompts, OS account recovery, and less master-password handling.
settings.credentialStorageEncryptedComparison: Encrypted database stores KKTerm secrets in SQLite protected by your master password. It works across platforms and can be unlocked with an environment variable, but anyone with the database and master password can decrypt it, and KKTerm cannot recover a lost password.
settings.credentialStorageEncryptedLinuxReminder: Linux uses encrypted database storage in this build. Protect your KKTerm database, use a long unique master password, and avoid putting KKTERM_SECRET_STORE_PASSWORD in shell history, logs, or shared startup scripts.
settings.credentialStorageSwitchNote: Switching stores does not move existing credentials. Save new credentials again if you want them available in the selected store.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreSetupBody: Enter the master password for the encrypted secrets database.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreSecurityTradeoff: Compared with the OS keystore, encrypted database storage is more portable but puts recovery and file protection on you. Keep the database private and do not expose the master password through scripts, logs, or shell history.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreLinuxSafety: On Linux, encrypted database storage is the available credential backend in this build. Keep the KKTerm database private, back it up carefully, and store the master password outside KKTerm.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreCreateBody: Create a master password for the encrypted secrets database.
settings.encryptedSecretStorePasswordGuidance: Use a long, unique password or passphrase and keep it somewhere safe. KKTerm cannot recover it if it is lost.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreEnvironmentHint: To avoid this prompt on app launch, set the KKTERM_SECRET_STORE_PASSWORD environment variable before starting KKTerm.
settings.encryptedSecretStorePasswordRequired: Enter the master password for encrypted database storage.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreResetBody: Create a new master password and clear the encrypted credentials that were saved with the previous password.
settings.encryptedSecretStoreResetWarning: Existing encrypted credentials will be invalidated and removed.
settings.widgetCredentialsHint: Credentials saved for Dashboard widget settings. Values stay in the selected secret store; widgets only keep references.
settings.deleteCredentialConfirmBody: Are you sure? This cannot be undone
settings.assistantSkillsHint: Local SKILL.md folders that teach the AI Assistant focused workflows. Bundled starter skills are copied here automatically. Scripts are not executed in this version; use the folder button to add or edit skills.
settings.assistantCustomSkillsHint: Load extra SKILL.md folders from assistant-skills\custom when the AI Assistant starts a request or when you refresh this list.
settings.mcpServersHint: Connect to remote Model Context Protocol servers so the AI assistant and Dashboard widgets can call their tools. KKTerm supports HTTP streamable MCP servers only; stdio configurations that spawn local processes are not supported.
settings.mcpDeleteConfirmBody: Remove the "{{name}}" MCP server? Its auth secret will be deleted from the keychain and widgets that reference this server will fail until reconfigured.
settings.mcpPasteHint: Paste an HTTP MCP server JSON config. Common shapes work: a single { "url": ..., "headers": ... } object or a Claude Desktop-style { "mcpServers": { "name": { ... } } } map.
settings.mcpConfirmHint: Review the parsed configuration before saving. KKTerm will probe the server's tool list immediately after creation.
settings.mcpDetectedSecretHint: An authentication header was detected. The token will be stored in the OS keychain; only a {SECRET} placeholder is persisted in the SQLite config.
settings.mcpAddInvalidJson: Could not parse JSON. Check that the pasted text is valid.
settings.mcpAddInvalidShape: Configuration must be a JSON object with at least a url field.
settings.mcpAddStdioUnsupported: This MCP server uses local process execution (stdio transport), which KKTerm does not support.
settings.mcpStdioGuidance: Look for an HTTP-based MCP endpoint from the same provider, or check their documentation for a hosted option.
settings.mcpErrorDuplicateName: An MCP server with that name already exists.
settings.mcpErrorKeychain: OS keychain is unavailable; secrets cannot be stored.
settings.screenshotFolderHint: Screenshots are saved here and the gallery loads images from this folder. Default: %USERPROFILE%\Pictures\Screenshots.
settings.resetLayoutDescription: Reset panel widths, collapsed panels, and saved terminal pane layouts.
settings.workspaceAccessHint: Controls quick access and local runtime permissions.
settings.performanceHint: Controls local rendering and screen capture acceleration.
settings.useDirectxScreenCaptureHint: Use DXGI Desktop Duplication when available, with GDI fallback.
settings.statusBarVisibleHint: Show the bottom bar with notifications and status indicators.
settings.statusBarMonitorHint: When off, KKTerm stops collecting host usage snapshots.
settings.autoBackupHint: Runs at startup; backups older than 1 week are removed.
settings.autoUpdateChecksHint: Checks GitHub Releases at startup and prompts you to download new versions.
settings.dontSleepForegroundOnly: Enable Don't Sleep only when app is in foreground
settings.dontSleepForegroundOnlyHint: When off, Don't Sleep keeps the OS awake globally while KKTerm is running.
settings.allowClipboardReadHint: Lets URL tabs read the clipboard for paste without the native prompt.
settings.autoStartWithWindowsHint: Launch KKTerm minimized when you sign in to Windows.
settings.minimizeToTrayHint: Keep KKTerm in the tray instead of quitting when minimized or closed.
settings.importSettingsConfirm: Importing settings closes open Sessions and replaces the settings database. Continue?
settings.resetAllSettingsConfirm: Reset all Settings pages to defaults, close open Sessions, reset saved layouts, clear the saved language override, and remove saved AI/email secrets?
settings.advancedDebuggingHint: Write full debug logs even in release builds. May include prompts, tool arguments, command output, and screenshots.
settings.rdpWebviewStabilityHint: Force software rendering so KKTerm stays responsive inside an RDP host across reconnects. Disables GPU acceleration, so leave it off for local installs. Restart to apply.
settings.importBackupFileHint: Choose a KKTerm backup (.kkbackup or .zip) to import.
settings.includeCredentialsHint: Connection, URL, and SOCKS proxy passwords.
settings.includeCredentialsWarning: Passwords are encrypted with the passphrase below. Anyone importing this file must enter the same passphrase. Without it, the export contains no passwords — safe to share.
settings.selectiveImportFileHint: Choose a KKTerm selective backup to import.
settings.fullBackupImportHint: Imports the complete KKTerm settings database and reloads the app.
settings.selectiveImportActionsHint: Choose what to do with each category
settings.selectiveImportWarning: A safety backup of your current data is created before importing. Replace clears that category first; Add keeps your existing items and adds the imported ones.
settings.segmentDesc_connections: SSH, URL, RDP, VNC and other saved Connections.
settings.segmentDesc_dashboards: Dashboard views, widget layouts, and custom widgets.
settings.sshAuthenticationHint: Default key material for new SSH Connections.
settings.sshTerminalHint: Scrollback and remote clipboard behavior for SSH Sessions.
settings.defaultKeyHint: Used by new key-based SSH Connections.
settings.defaultSshUserHint: Pre-fills new SSH and Telnet Connections.
settings.defaultSshPortHint: Used when a Connection omits a port.
settings.defaultSshPortRange: Default SSH port must be between 1 and 65535.
settings.sshBufferHint: Default 5,000; used for SSH scrollback and tmux pane history.
settings.defaultTransparencyHint: Used for new terminal Connections and new Child Connection Tabs.
settings.defaultTransparencyRange: Default transparency must be between 0 and 100.
settings.randomDynamicBackgroundOnCreateHint: When on, new terminal Connections, new tabs, and new Child Connection Tabs start with a random dynamic background.
settings.sshBufferRange: SSH buffer must be between 100 and 100000 lines.
settings.portRedirectHint: Choose which remote loopback ports appear in the SSH terminal menu.
settings.xServerHint: Start VcXsrv before SSH Sessions that need local X11 windows.
settings.xServerManagedHint: When on, KKTerm starts VcXsrv if it is not already running before opening an SSH Session.
settings.xServerPathHint: Leave blank to use the standard VcXsrv install locations.
settings.xServerDisplayRange: X server display must be between 0 and 99.
settings.sshKeyEmailPrompt: Email for the SSH key comment:
settings.sshKeyEmailDialogHint: Use an email comment so the key is easy to recognize.
settings.proxyJumpHint: Optional ProxyJump host for new SSH Connections.
settings.sshSocksProxyHint: Optional SOCKS proxy for SSH Connections, applied to terminals, tmux, and SFTP. Use the username and password fields for authenticated proxies; username:password@host:port is still accepted in the address field if you do not need encrypted password storage.
settings.sshSocksProxyUsernameHint: Optional username for authenticated SOCKS proxies.
settings.sshSocksProxyPasswordSavedPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep saved password
settings.sshSocksProxyPasswordHint: Stored in the configured secret store when a proxy address and username are set.
settings.sshCompressionHint: Default transport compression for SSH Connections (matches ssh -XC). Fast is recommended for X11 forwarding and slow links; individual Connections can override this.
settings.terminalTextHint: Local terminal font, size, line height, and cursor.
settings.terminalSessionHint: Default shell and scrollback for local terminal Sessions.
settings.terminalClipboardHint: Local terminal copy, OSC 52, and paste safety.
settings.customShellsHint: Add local shell profiles to reuse in the default shell picker and Local terminal Connections.
settings.rdpDisplayHint: Planned display defaults for new RDP Connections.
settings.rdpNetworkHint: Planned network tuning for new RDP Connections.
settings.rdpResolutionHint: Maps to DesktopWidth, DesktopHeight, SmartSizing, and sync.
settings.rdpBandwidthProfileHint: Uses bandwidth detection instead of a raw bitrate cap.
settings.rdpPerformanceFlagsHint: Controls wallpaper, drag, animations, themes, cursors, and font smoothing.
settings.rdpRedirectClipboardHint: Allow clipboard copy and paste between KKTerm and the remote desktop.
settings.rdpRedirectDrivesHint: Expose local drives to the remote desktop session.
settings.rdpEnhancedGraphicsHint: Uses RDP performance flags on capable servers.
settings.vncEncodingHint: Planned encoding and compression defaults for VNC Connections.
settings.vncDisplayHint: Planned display behavior for VNC Connections.
settings.vncQualityHint: Adapts encoding and pixel format to link speed.
settings.vncPreferredEncodingHint: Also advertises CopyRect, Raw, cursor, and desktop-size support.
settings.vncCompressionHint: Higher values reduce bandwidth at a CPU cost.
settings.vncColorLevelHint: Can drop to 256, 64, or 8 colors on slower links.
settings.vncSharedSessionHint: Allow another VNC viewer to stay connected when KKTerm opens this session.
settings.vncViewOnlyHint: Connect without sending pointer or keyboard input.
settings.vncRemoteResizeHint: Depends on the VNC server supporting desktop-size updates.
settings.updateAvailableBody: KKTerm {{version}} is available. You are running {{currentVersion}}.
settings.updateDownloadStarting: Downloading the KKTerm installer. KKTerm will close before installation starts.
settings.updateDownloadFailed: Could not download and install the update: {{message}}
settings.updateChecksRequireRuntime: Update checks require the Tauri desktop runtime.
settings.appSlogan: The terminal tool for keeping every machine on its best behavior.
settings.customFontsHint: Press the refresh button to get system fonts. To use custom fonts, put them in the fonts folder.
settings.noCustomFonts: No custom fonts found. Add .ttf, .otf, .woff, or .woff2 files to the app fonts folder.
settings.systemFontsUnavailable: System font detection isn't available in this app build.
settings.fontSizeRange: Terminal font size must be between 8 and 32.
settings.lineHeightRange: Terminal line height must be between 1.0 and 2.0.
settings.scrollbackRange: Terminal scrollback must be between 100 and 100000 lines.
settings.appFontFamilyRequired: App UI font family is required.
settings.secretSaveFailed: Couldn't save the credential to the secret store: {{error}}
settings.keychainSaveFailedTitle: Couldn't save to the secret store
settings.keychainResetHint: Reset removes the stored entry and saves again, which asks the operating system to grant access. On macOS, choose Allow when the keychain prompt appears.
settings.showAllModelsHint: Show every provider model returned by refresh instead of only the curated recommendations.
settings.clearAiConfirm: Reset AI provider settings and remove the saved API key?
settings.dashboardLayoutEnforcementHint: How strictly KKTerm forces AI-created widgets to fill their frame. Strict makes the widget body act as the frame so content cannot collapse into a small centered card; Moderate keeps the standard behavior; Low gives the most freedom and lets widgets size to their content.
settings.dashboardMaxActiveScriptWidgetsHint: Maximum script widgets that render their iframe at once on a Dashboard. Excess widgets show a click-to-activate placeholder. Allowed range {{min}}–{{max}}.
settings.dashboardUseRandomDynamicBackgroundDesc: New Dashboard Views automatically start with a random dynamic background.
settings.dashboardAllowWidgetNetworkToolsDesc: When enabled, script widgets that request permissions.networkTools can run ping, port scan, DNS, WHOIS, and Wake-on-LAN. Disable to globally block these from all widgets.
settings.hideTopTabButtons: Use Child Connection Tabs in the Connection Tree
settings.hideTopTabButtonsDesc: When enabled, the top Tab Strip is hidden and saved Child Connection Tabs appear as italic rows under their parent Connection.
settings.doubleClickOpensConnectionDesc: When enabled, single-clicking a Connection in the Connection Tree only selects it and double-clicking opens it. Use the right-click Rename action to rename Connections.
settings.fileExplorerOpenModeHint: Choose whether File Explorer opens local files inside KKTerm or with the operating system's default app.
settings.fileExplorerTerminalHint: Choose the shell used by the File Explorer terminal button. Custom shells are managed in Terminal settings.
settings.submitAiAttachmentsDirectly: Submit screenshot/text to AI Assistant directly
settings.submitAiAttachmentsDirectlyDesc: When enabled, Send to AI Assistant submits the attached screenshot or terminal buffer with a default help request. When disabled, it only attaches the context so you can type your own question.
settings.separateSplitTerminalBackgrounds: Separate background for each terminal in split panes
settings.separateSplitTerminalBackgroundsDesc: When off, one background fills the terminal workspace content area for a Connection Tab. When on, split terminal panes can keep separate backgrounds.
settings.installerShowOnRailDesc: When enabled, the Install Helper icon appears on the Activity Rail.
settings.installerCheckIntervalDesc: How often the Install Helper automatically checks for the latest available versions when you open it. Use Refresh to check immediately.
settings.ignoreCertificateErrorsHint: Allows self-signed or untrusted certificates in new URL tabs.
settings.urlProxyHint: Default network route for new URL Connection tabs. HTTP proxies tunnel both HTTP and HTTPS destinations.
settings.urlProxyPlatformHint: Applied when a URL tab opens. macOS proxy support requires macOS 14 or later.
settings.savedWebsitePasswordsHint: Passwords stay in the OS keychain; metadata stays in SQLite for Autofill.
settings.urlCredentialPasswordPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current password
settings.urlDataShardsHint: Clearing a shard removes its partition label from matching URL Connections.
settings.aiProviderConnectionHint: Endpoint, model, reasoning, and API key.
settings.useCodexCliHint: Use the local Codex CLI as the OpenAI backend. The API key field is disabled; Codex CLI authentication is used instead.
settings.useClaudeCliHint: Use the local Claude Code CLI as the Anthropic backend. The API key field is disabled; Claude Code authentication is used instead.
settings.aiCliStatusUnknown: CLI status has not been checked.
settings.aiCliStatusAuthRequired: CLI is installed but not authenticated.
settings.aiCliInstallFailed: AI CLI installation did not complete.
settings.aiCliAuthStarted: AI CLI authentication opened in an external terminal.
settings.copilotConnectionHint: Uses GitHub device sign-in. The returned token stays in the OS keychain.
settings.copilotCliStatusUnknown: Copilot CLI status has not been checked.
settings.copilotCliStatusMissing: Copilot CLI not found. Install it or set COPILOT_CLI_PATH.
settings.aiAllowInsecureTls: Allow insecure TLS for AI providers
settings.aiAllowInsecureTlsHint: Ignore invalid or self-signed certificates for AI provider requests. Use only for trusted local or internal endpoints.
settings.aiCustomInstructionsHint: Optional Assistant guidance, up to {{count}} characters. KKTerm safety, approval, and local-first constraints still apply.
settings.aiCustomInstructionsTooLong: AI Assistant custom instructions must be {{count}} characters or fewer.
settings.aiToolsDescription: Choose the built-in tools the AI Assistant may call.
settings.aiToolsSafety: Safeguards: file tools stay in app data, shell commands run from app data, and destructive requests are blocked.

currentTime.label: Current time
currentTime.description: Get local time in RFC 3339 format.

performanceCounters.label: Performance counters
performanceCounters.description: Read low-overhead local CPU, memory, network, process, uptime, and drive-space counters.

webSearch.label: Web search
webSearch.description: Return compact titles, links, and snippets.

webFetch.label: Fetch web page
webFetch.description: Read and summarize one http/https page.

appDataFileSearch.label: Search app data files
appDataFileSearch.description: Find files inside app data only.

appDataFileRead.label: Read app data file
appDataFileRead.description: Read small text files in app data only.

shellCommand.label: PowerShell / batch commands
shellCommand.description: Run non-destructive commands from app data only.

email.label: Send Email
email.description: Send reviewed messages through Resend, SendGrid, Mailgun, Postmark, or SMTP.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Read and modify Dashboard views, widget instances, and AI Created Widgets.

connections.label: Connections
connections.description: List, create, edit, and delete saved Connections.

sessions.label: Live Sessions
sessions.description: Read visible session context and interact with active terminal, remote desktop, and file browser Sessions.

tutorial.label: Tutorial
tutorial.description: Highlight visible app controls, dim the rest of the window, and show one-step balloon help.

manual.label: Operation Manual
manual.description: Search and read the bundled KKTerm Operation Manual to answer how-to questions about app features.

network.label: Network admin tools
network.description: Ping hosts, check TCP ports, resolve DNS, run WHOIS, list interfaces, and send Wake-on-LAN.

memory.label: Assistant memory
memory.description: Let the assistant save and recall short notes about your hosts and preferences across chats. Never stores secrets.

settings.connectedConnectionsRailHint: Adds connected Connection icons to the Activity Rail.
settings.connectedConnectionsRailSaved: Show Connection in Left Rail setting saved.
settings.useCustomTitleBarHint: Replace the Windows native title bar with KKTerm's themed bar. The change takes effect immediately on save.
settings.builtInMcpServerEnabledHint: Expose kkterm-cli as a stdio MCP server for external tools.
settings.builtInMcpAllowAllDangerous: Built-in MCP dangerous tools: Allow all
settings.builtInMcpAllowAllDangerousHint: When enabled, MCP tools with a dangerous namespace segment execute without confirmation prompts.
settings.allowInsecureMcpHttp: Allow insecure Remote MCP HTTP servers
settings.allowInsecureMcpHttpHint: When enabled, Remote MCP servers on local or network http:// URLs are allowed; leave this off unless you trust the network and server.
settings.builtInMcpConfigIntro: Paste one of these snippets into an MCP-capable tool, or use a setup command below. The command path uses the kkterm-cli binary beside your running KKTerm app.
settings.unsavedQuitBody: Save your changes before closing Settings?
settings.itOpsShowModuleDesc: Adds the IT Ops Module (Host Groups, Batch Runs, Automations) to the Activity Rail. This Module is under active development and is off by default.

connections.title: Connections
connections.openOneOffSession: Quick connect — saved to your connections
connections.localStartupScriptPlaceholder: Optional commands to run after launch
connections.sshStartupScriptEmpty: No startup script yet — open the editor to add one.
connections.sshStartupScriptEditorHint: These commands are typed into the remote shell each time a session opens.
connections.sshStartupScriptApplyExistingTmux: Apply to existing tmux sessions on attach
connections.sshStartupScriptApplyExistingTmuxDesc: Run the script again when re-attaching to a running tmux session, not only when it is first created.
connections.sshStartupScriptAiPromptLabel: Describe what the startup script should do
connections.sshStartupScriptAiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. activate the project virtualenv and cd into the app
connections.environmentVariablesUnsupportedShell: Environment variables support Command Prompt, PowerShell, WSL, Bash, and zsh.
connections.environmentVariablesHint: These variables are set when this Connection starts.
connections.environmentVariablesNonSecret: Values are stored in the Startup script. Do not store passwords, tokens, or API keys here.
connections.environmentVariablesMalformed: KKTerm cannot safely edit the existing managed environment block. Fix or remove it in the Startup script first.
connections.environmentVariableInvalidName: Names must start with a letter or underscore and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.
connections.environmentVariableDuplicateName: Each variable name must be unique.
connections.environmentVariableMultilineValue: Values must stay on one line.
connections.cliAccountAlternateProfileHint: Set AI Coding Agent alternate account profile
connections.cliAccountUnsupportedShell: Alternate CLI account profiles support Command Prompt, PowerShell, WSL, Bash, and zsh.
connections.cliAccountHint: Choose one CLI account. KKTerm sets an isolated data directory; sign in normally after opening the terminal.
connections.cliAccountInvalidLabel: Enter an account label containing a letter or number.
connections.urlProxyOverrideHint: Inherit the URL Settings default, force a direct connection, or choose a proxy for this Connection.
connections.leaveBlankPassword: Leave blank to keep stored password
connections.passwordOptionalHint: Leave blank to enter the password in the terminal
connections.sshSocksProxyHint: Routes this Connection's terminals, tmux, and SFTP through a SOCKS proxy natively. Cannot be combined with ProxyJump.
connections.sshSocksProxyPasswordPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep saved password
connections.keyPassphraseHint: Stored in the configured secret store. Leave blank to be prompted when the key requires one.
connections.leaveBlankPassphrase: Leave blank to keep the saved passphrase
connections.vncUsernameHint: Optional. Use the Mac account name for macOS Screen Sharing / Apple Remote Desktop login. Leave blank for password-only VNC.
connections.transferSshPublicKeyHint: Transfers only the selected public key to the remote user's authorized_keys using password authentication.
connections.transferSshPublicKeyProxyJumpUnsupported: TransferSSH pub key does not support ProxyJump connections yet.

terminal.replaceChangedHostKeyWarning: The SSH host key for {{host}} has changed, and now presents {{algorithm}} {{fingerprint}}. This usually means the server was reinstalled or rotated its key, but it can also indicate a man-in-the-middle attack. Only replace the trusted key if you expected this change.

watchdogExamples: alert if CPU over 90% for 5 min
alert when RAM usage over 80%
alert when disk usage over 85%
alert if disk free drops below 10%
alert when commit memory over 90%
alert if process count spikes over 800
alert when handle count exceeds 50000
alert if KKTerm RAM over 1 GB
alert when network upstream over 50 MB/s
let me know when CPU drops back under 50%
ping my-server.example.com — alert if it goes offline
monitor 192.168.1.10 and notify if it stops responding
alert if SSH on my-host stops accepting connections
alert if port 443 on api.example.com closes
alert if my database port 5432 stops listening
watch port 8080 — tell me when it becomes available again
watch my codex CLI session — nudge it if silent for 60 seconds
watch this SSH session for 2 minutes of silence then notify me
monitor my long-running build — alert if output stops for 5 min
if codex hangs on a prompt, send it /continue automatically
ai.toolApprovalBody: This action can change KKTerm or the local machine.

watchdogApproval.allowedToolsCaveat: The AI may call any of these without further prompts during this watchdog's lifetime. Other tools are refused at runtime.
watchdogApproval.caps: Hard cap: {{max}} interventions max · {{suppression}}s cooldown between actions
